title: The firewall paradox (AMPS)
statement: Almheiri, Marolf, Polchinski and Sully (2012) argued that three widely-held assumptions cannot all be true for an old (post-Page-time) black hole: (i) evaporation is unitary, (ii) effective field theory is valid outside the horizon, and (iii) an infalling observer sees nothing special at the horizon (the equivalence principle / 'no drama'). Monogamy of entanglement forces a contradiction; the proposed escape is that the horizon is replaced by a high-energy 'firewall' that destroys infalling observers, violating the equivalence principle. Whether firewalls exist, or which assumption truly fails, is open.

ranking.rationale: Newer (2012) than the deepest problems, but it sharpened the information paradox into a precise contradiction and reshaped the field; ranked here because it is a crisp, actively-attacked statement rather than a decades-old impasse.

key_ideas: Monogamy of entanglement: a late Hawking quantum cannot be maximally entangled with both its early-radiation partner (required by unitarity) and its interior partner (required by a smooth horizon).
Page time: the argument bites only for 'old' black holes that have radiated more than half their entropy.
Firewall: dropping the smooth-horizon assumption replaces the horizon with a wall of high-energy quanta.
Mathur's earlier 'small corrections' theorem reached a related conclusion via fuzzballs.
ER=EPR (problem #08) was proposed partly to dissolve the firewall by identifying the two entanglements geometrically.
The island/replica resolution of #02 bears directly on whether a firewall is required.

approaches.name: Firewalls
approaches.summary: Accept that old black-hole horizons are not smooth; the interior is cut off by a high-energy surface.

approaches.name: ER=EPR / state-dependence
approaches.summary: Identify interior and exterior partners so monogamy is not violated; interior operators depend on the state.

approaches.name: Computational complexity / firewall avoidance
approaches.summary: Decoding the interior is exponentially hard, so a firewall is never operationally encountered (Harlow-Hayden).

approaches.name: Fuzzballs
approaches.summary: There is no smooth interior at all; the horizon is the surface of a stringy bound state.

papers.title: Black holes: complementarity or firewalls?
papers.note: The original AMPS paper that posed the firewall paradox: the three statements (purity of Hawking radiation, information emitted near the horizon with valid EFT, and a smooth horizon for the infalling observer) cannot all hold. JHEP 02 (2013) 062. ~1695 citations.

papers.title: An apologia for firewalls
papers.note: The AMPSS follow-up defending and sharpening the firewall argument against proposed alternatives, including the typical-state / large-AdS-black-hole version. JHEP 09 (2013) 018.

papers.title: The stretched horizon and black hole complementarity
papers.note: Foundational statement of black hole complementarity and the stretched horizon, the framework AMPS later challenged. Phys. Rev. D 48 (1993) 3743.

papers.title: Complementarity is not enough
papers.note: Argues that causal-patch complementarity cannot reconcile the conflicting entanglement requirements, reinforcing the firewall conclusion via monogamy. Phys. Rev. D 87 (2013) 124023.

papers.title: Complementarity, not Firewalls
papers.note: Early rebuttal arguing a version of complementarity evades firewalls by restricting EFT to a single observer's experiments.

papers.title: Black hole entanglement and quantum error correction
papers.note: Proposes reconstructing interior operators via quantum error correction on the stretched horizon to avoid the firewall below the Bekenstein-Hawking bound. JHEP 10 (2013) 107.

papers.title: Quantum computation vs. firewalls
papers.note: The Harlow-Hayden argument: decoding the early radiation to expose a firewall is computationally intractable (time exponential in the entropy), suggesting the experiment cannot be done before the black hole evaporates. JHEP 06 (2013) 085.

papers.title: Cool horizons for entangled black holes (ER=EPR)
papers.note: The ER=EPR conjecture: entanglement is geometrized as Einstein-Rosen bridges, offering a proposed resolution of the AMPS(S) firewall paradoxes. Fortsch. Phys. 61 (2013) 781. ~1607 citations.

papers.title: Violations of the Born rule in cool state-dependent horizons
papers.note: Shows that state-dependent constructions strong enough to remove firewalls from generic states require massive Born-rule violations, a key objection to the state-dependence resolution. JHEP 01 (2016) 008.

papers.title: Aspects of the Papadodimas-Raju proposal for the black hole interior
papers.note: Analyzes the Papadodimas-Raju state-dependent mirror-operator construction of the interior and its conceptual tensions with ordinary quantum mechanics. JHEP 11 (2014) 055.

papers.title: The information paradox: a pedagogical introduction (inadequacy of small corrections)
papers.note: Proves the 'small corrections theorem': small corrections to the leading Hawking computation cannot remove the radiation-interior entanglement, sharpening the paradox the firewall responds to. Class. Quantum Grav. 26 (2009) 224001. ~979 citations.

papers.title: The fuzzball proposal for black holes: an elementary review
papers.note: Foundational review of the fuzzball picture: horizon-scale microstate structure replaces the smooth horizon, a structure-at-the-horizon alternative to the firewall. Fortsch. Phys. 53 (2005) 793. ~1071 citations.

papers.title: The fuzzball proposal for black holes
papers.note: Comprehensive Physics Reports review of the fuzzball proposal and its tests via AdS/CFT for the D1-D5 system. Phys. Rept. 467 (2008) 117.

papers.title: Fuzzballs and Microstate Geometries: Black-Hole Structure in String Theory (Snowmass White Paper)
papers.note: Recent Snowmass-era review of the fuzzball and microstate-geometry programs as the string-theoretic resolution of the information/firewall problem.

papers.title: Multipartite Entanglement and Firewalls
papers.note: Uses multipartite entanglement (negativity) in a toy model to question the AMPS monogamy accounting and whether a firewall is required.

papers.title: HPS meets AMPS: how soft hair dissolves the firewall
papers.note: Proposes that Hawking-Perry-Strominger soft hair encodes a code-subspace projection allowing interior reconstruction, with the firewall receding for the infalling observer. JHEP 09 (2021) 099.

papers.title: The Python's Lunch: geometric obstructions to decoding Hawking radiation
papers.note: Traces the Harlow-Hayden computational hardness of decoding the radiation to a geometric obstruction (non-minimal quantum extremal surfaces) in the wormhole. JHEP 08 (2020) 121.

papers.title: Entanglement wedge reconstruction and the information paradox
papers.note: Shows a quantum-extremal-surface phase transition at the Page time recovers the Page curve without a firewall, and argues non-perturbative reconstruction errors evade the AMPSS typical-state paradox. JHEP 09 (2020) 002. ~895 citations.

papers.title: The Page curve of Hawking radiation from semiclassical geometry (islands)
papers.note: Introduces the 'island' rule and shows the radiation entropy follows the Page curve, with the interior in the radiation's entanglement wedge in the spirit of ER=EPR. JHEP 03 (2020) 149.

papers.title: Replica wormholes and the entropy of Hawking radiation
papers.note: Derives the island rule from new replica-wormhole saddles in the gravitational path integral, restoring unitarity of the Page curve. JHEP 05 (2020) 013. ~756 citations.

papers.title: Observations of Hawking radiation: the Page curve and baby universes
papers.note: Reformulates replica-wormhole results for asymptotic observers, finding unitary black-hole statistics at the cost of baby-universe superselection sectors. JHEP 04 (2021) 272.

papers.title: Lessons from the information paradox
papers.note: Physics Reports review covering the refined interior paradox, state-dependence, and the viability of firewalls, fuzzballs, and other horizon structure. Phys. Rept. 943 (2022) 1.

papers.title: The black hole information problem: past, present, and future
papers.note: Overview of the information problem emphasizing fundamental issues and unitarity-restoring resolutions, with the firewall puzzle central. Rept. Prog. Phys. 80 (2017) 092001.

papers.title: The Black Hole Information Problem
papers.note: Pedagogical TASI-style review by a firewall originator: Hawking radiation, the Page curve, complementarity and its limitations, and the firewall and proposed 'drama.'

papers.title: Particle Creation by Black Holes
papers.note: The foundational result that black holes emit thermal radiation, originating the information paradox the firewall addresses. Commun. Math. Phys. 43 (1975) 199. Canonical landmark cited from certain knowledge.

papers.title: Black Holes and the Second Law (and Black Hole Entropy)
papers.note: Introduced black hole entropy proportional to horizon area, foundational for the Page-curve / unitarity tension underlying the firewall debate. Phys. Rev. D 7 (1973) 2333 (entropy) and Lett. Nuovo Cimento 4 (1972) 737. Canonical landmark cited from certain knowledge.

physicists.name: Joseph Polchinski
physicists.contribution: Co-author of the original AMPS firewall paper (2012) and its apologia, framing the paradox as an inconsistency between unitarity, EFT, and a smooth horizon.

physicists.name: Ahmed Almheiri
physicists.affiliation: NYU Abu Dhabi / Institute for Advanced Study
physicists.contribution: Lead author of the AMPS firewall paper and a principal architect of the island rule and replica-wormhole resolution of the Page curve.

physicists.name: Donald Marolf
physicists.contribution: AMPS(S) co-author; sharpened the typical-state firewall argument and showed state-dependence requires Born-rule violations; co-developed replica wormholes for asymptotic observers.

physicists.name: James Sully
physicists.contribution: Co-author of the original AMPS and AMPSS firewall papers.

physicists.name: Leonard Susskind
physicists.contribution: Co-originated black hole complementarity and the stretched horizon, and co-proposed ER=EPR with Maldacena as a route around the firewall.

physicists.name: Samir D. Mathur
physicists.contribution: Proved the small-corrections theorem and developed the fuzzball proposal, the leading horizon-structure alternative anticipating firewall-like physics.

physicists.name: Daniel Harlow
physicists.contribution: With Hayden, formulated the computational-complexity (Harlow-Hayden) obstruction to decoding the radiation, and analyzed quantum error correction and state-dependence in the interior.

physicists.name: Patrick Hayden
physicists.contribution: Co-author of the Harlow-Hayden complexity argument and of the Hayden-Preskill black-hole-as-mirror information-retrieval result underlying the firewall setup.

physicists.name: Juan Maldacena
physicists.contribution: Co-proposed ER=EPR with Susskind and co-developed the island / quantum-extremal-surface program recovering the unitary Page curve.

physicists.name: Raphael Bousso
physicists.contribution: Argued in 'Complementarity is not enough' that causal-patch complementarity fails to evade the firewall, strengthening the monogamy-based case.

simulator_angle: No direct classical-GR observable (the firewall is a quantum-gravity feature at the horizon), but the Page-time setup uses the same Hawking temperature/evaporation the simulator's thermo module computes.

metadata.coverage_note: Assembled 25 verified papers and 10 physicists. Method: ~11 Consensus MCP searches (mcp__plugin_bio-research_consensus__search) across the themes — AMPS/complementarity, monogamy of entanglement, Harlow-Hayden complexity, fuzzballs/small-corrections, ER=EPR, Page curve/islands/replica wormholes, state-dependence (Papadodimas-Raju), black hole complementarity originals, and recent reviews. 23 of the 25 papers carry a Consensus URL plus arXiv id and/or DOI verified from the returned metadata; the remaining 2 are canonical landmarks cited from certain knowledge with bibliographic notes only (Hawking 1975, Commun. Math. Phys. 43:199, DOI 10.1007/BF02345020; Bekenstein 1973, Phys. Rev. D 7:2333, DOI 10.1103/PhysRevD.7.2333) — no URL invented for these. The Harlow-Hayden paper appears under its actual title 'Quantum computation vs. firewalls' (arXiv:1301.4504). Tiers reflect that this is an OPEN problem: nearly all resolution-level entries are open_conjecture or speculative_extension, while exact-GR foundations (Hawking, Bekenstein, Mathur's theorem) are analytic_classical. Two original works not surfaced by search and therefore omitted to avoid fabrication: Hayden-Preskill 2007 ('Black holes as mirrors', arXiv:0708.4025) and the original Papadodimas-Raju 'An Infalling Observer in AdS/CFT' (arXiv:1211.6767) — both are represented indirectly via verified derivative/analysis papers. No titles, authors, years, arXiv ids, DOIs, or URLs were invented.
